page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7se.3xlarge is a general-purpose virtual server instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 12 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 12 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 48.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations place the instance consistently in the average performance tier across multiple workloads. The Intel Xeon processor delivers a single-core stress-ng score of 1862.99 and a multi-core score of 11645.18. Memory bandwidth tests show 629.81 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, dropping to 83.06 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. In database operations, the instance achieves 11.94 million operations per second on Redis SET tests and a Passmark database score of 5988.1. For language model tasks, the server processes 1071.79 tokens per second for a small 135M model, but performance drops to a weak 24.26 tokens per second for prompt processing on a medium 7B model.

From a resource density perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation and 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio offer a predictable hardware profile for general-purpose workloads. However, the lack of bundled local storage requires users to attach external block storage, which may impact overall I/O latency and cost. The absence of a GPU limits its utility for heavy machine learning training. This instance is best suited for workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, text processing, and moderate database operations.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7se.3xlarge is a general-purpose virtual server featuring 12 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor at 2.9 GHz, paired with 48.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates average performance across standard compute and memory tasks, with a single-core stress-ng score of 1862.99 and a multi-core score of 11645.18. The instance demonstrates solid capabilities in database operations, achieving 11.94 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks, and static web serving at 17.05 GB/sec. However, it exhibits weak performance in prompt processing for medium 7B language models. This server is designed for balanced workloads such as web serving, software compilation, and moderate database hosting where dedicated CPU resources are required without local storage.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7se.3xlarge is a general-purpose virtual server with 12 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 48 GB RAM, and 4 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering average benchmark performance for database and web workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7se.3xlarge features 12 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 48 GB RAM, KVM virtualization, and average performance for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A dedicated twelve-core x86_64 virtual server with balanced memory allocation and baseline network throughput for general-purpose enterprise workloads.

48.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM virtualization
No bundled local storage or GPU hardware included
Average performance in database operations and web serving
Best suited for software compilation, text processing, and moderate databases
